title: Tapping to Feel Joy in Circulating Money
subtitle: A tapping practice for shifting from dread at spending to ease with money in motion
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 59 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the contraction that comes with every payment — the sense that a balance dropping is loss, not flow. The reframe moves toward money circulation as the active sign of a working system.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script is adapted from Day 59 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you've just made a payment and feel smaller for it, or before a planned purchase that feels tense despite being affordable.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 59 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the contraction pattern around spending: money leaving feels like loss, the balance dropping reads as bad news, every payment carries a slight dread. The reframe moves toward money in motion as the active evidence of a circulating system — money that moves and comes back.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I once worried every expense was loss, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I thought money leaving was bad, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I felt tense when spending, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I used to see every payment as a minus.
Side of eye: I felt smaller with each bill.
Under eye: I dreaded watching balances drop.
Under nose: I tried to hold on too tightly.
Chin: I forgot that money moves and returns.
Collarbone: I'm ready to enjoy the flow.
Under arm: I welcome money as a living cycle.
Top of head: I trust money to come and go with ease.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I celebrate money in motion.
Side of eye: Every spend seeds future income.
Under eye: I trust circulation to bring more.
Under nose: I enjoy sending money out with ease.
Chin: I bless each pound as it flows back multiplied.
Collarbone: I relax and let wealth move naturally.
Under arm: I feel calm as money cycles through life.
Top of head: I live in steady, generous cash flow.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 59 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
